title: research: assess mise en place article for academic conversion
assembly: merge sections, references, figures, and verify page count
review: build final PDF and write REVIEW-NOTES.md
infra: set up ACM LaTeX template with section scaffolding
description:
## Context
Paper uses ACM Primary Article Template for VibeX 2026 workshop (co-located with EASE 2026).

## Task
Set up paper/ directory with acmart template. Create main.tex with full section scaffolding matching the plan (6 sections + references). Include placeholder content indicating what goes in each section. Set up references.bib with known citations. Create figures/ directory.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] paper/main.tex compiles to valid PDF with acmart sigconf,review class
- [ ] All 6 sections scaffolded with placeholder text
- [ ] paper/references.bib with key citations from literature survey
- [ ] paper/figures/ directory created
- [ ] Correct ACM conference metadata for EASE 2026 / VibeX

title: research: analyze VibeX 2026 CFP and submission requirements
writing: draft Methodology and Case Study sections
description:
## Context
Methodology (Section 3, ~1000 words) and Case Study (Section 4, ~800 words) are the paper's core. Draws from published article (.claude/ref/published-article.md) and extracted data (data/*.md).

## Task
Write LaTeX for Section 3 (three-phase mise en place methodology: contextual grounding, collaborative specification, task decomposition) and Section 4 (case study of The Beat hackathon with quantitative data). Include Table 1 (timeline breakdown) and describe Figure 1 (methodology diagram).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] paper/sections/methodology.tex with formalized three-phase methodology (~1000 words)
- [ ] paper/sections/case-study.tex with hackathon case study and quantitative data (~800 words)
- [ ] Table 1 defined in case-study.tex
- [ ] Figure 1 referenced/described in methodology.tex

title: plan: propose submission strategy and paper scaffold for VibeX 2026
research: survey related academic work on agentic coding methodologies
infra: extract quantitative data from hackathon repo
description:
## Context
The paper needs precise quantitative data from the hackathon project at ~/hackathon-infactory/. This repo is READ-ONLY.

## Task
Extract git timeline, beads metrics, file counts, and session analysis from the hackathon repo. Save results to ~/cfp/mise/data/.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] data/git-timeline.md: exact timestamps of all commits, commit frequency, total duration
- [ ] data/beads-metrics.md: total beads created/closed/open, priority distribution, dependency depth
- [ ] data/session-analysis.md: session count, line counts, phase transition timing, artifact word counts

title: writing: draft Related Work section
description:
## Context
Background and Related Work section (~600 words, 0.75 pages). Draws from .claude/research/literature-survey.md.

## Task
Write LaTeX for Section 2 covering: AI-assisted coding workflows (Copilot studies, security concerns), emerging preparation methodologies (RPI, SDD, Context Engineering), practitioner innovations (Ralph Loop, Beads, beads_rust), theoretical foundations (backward design, tacit knowledge). Identify the gap: no formalized methodology for preparation-phase work.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] paper/sections/related-work.tex with complete LaTeX content
- [ ] ~600 words, properly cited with \cite{} commands
- [ ] All citations have corresponding entries added to references.bib

title: writing: draft Intro, Context Fluency, Research Agenda, Conclusion
description:
## Context
Introduction (~400 words), Context Fluency (~400 words), Research Agenda + Conclusion (~400 words). These frame the paper and propose novel constructs.

## Task
Write LaTeX for: Section 1 (alignment problem, vibe coding, mise en place metaphor, contributions list), Section 5 (context fluency as emerging skill, components, distinction from prompt engineering, teaching connection), Section 6 (5 research questions with theoretical justification, concluding invitation).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] paper/sections/introduction.tex (~400 words)
- [ ] paper/sections/context-fluency.tex (~400 words)
- [ ] paper/sections/research-agenda.tex (~400 words)
- [ ] All properly cited with \cite{} commands
